Flow Straight and Fast: Learning to Generate and Transfer Data with Rectified Flow

Xingchao Liu, Chengyue Gong, Qiang Liu
September 2022
Abstract

We present rectified flow.

1 Introduction

Rectified flow is an ODE model11The map is causal. that transports \pi_{0} to \pi_{1}, following [1]. See Eq. (1) and Figure 1. Code at github.com/gnobitab/RectifiedFlow uses rectified_flow().

\mathrm{d}Z_{t}=v(Z_{t},t)\,\mathrm{d}t (1)

1.1 Contributions

We show straightness reduces cost.

2 Method

We optimize the reflow objective.

v^{*}=\arg\min_{v}\mathbb{E}\|(X_{1}-X_{0})-v\|^{2} (2)
Z_{0}=X_{0} (3)
  • •

    Straight paths lower discretization error.

  • •

    Reflow can be iterated per Eq. (1).

The velocity field is learned by regression.

Theorem 1.

Rectified flow does not increase transport cost.

pip install rectified-flow

Appendix A Proofs

See Theorem 1 for details.

References

  • Liu, X., Gong, C., Liu, Q. (2022). "Flow Straight and Fast: Learning to Generate and Transfer Data with Rectified Flow". arXiv:2209.03003.
  • Ho, J., Jain, A., Abbeel, P. (2020). "Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models". https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2006.11239.
